Today is February 11, 2026. Following the mass shooting in Tumbler Ridge, British Columbia, Mayor Darryl Krakowka spoke to the media about the impact on the community. How did he specifically describe the relationship between himself and the people of the town?
(A) He stated that he does not call them residents, but rather calls them family.
(B) He described them as a tight-knit group of survivors who have lived there for 19 years.
(C) He referred to them as his constituents and fellow remote community members.
(D) He said they were a small community of 2,500 people who are all like brothers and sisters.
(E) He noted that because he has been there for nearly two decades, he considers every victim a close personal friend.
explanation: In the article, Mayor Darryl Krakowka is quoted as saying, "I don't call them residents. I call them family."
model_output:
Based on the mayor's direct quotes from a recent news article, he described the community as "one big family" and specifically said, "It’s not residents. We’re a big family."[[1]](https://globalnews.ca/news/11662863/tumbler-ridge-shooting-mayor-calls-for-support) This aligns most closely with option (A).

<final_answer>
(A)
</final_answer>

CITATIONS:
- Global News (https://globalnews.ca/news/11662863/tumbler-ridge-shooting-mayor-calls-for-support)

Today is February 11, 2026. During the recent Super Bowl, several high-profile advertisements for Artificial Intelligence were aired. According to the report, what was the specific slogan used at the end of the commercial for the AI assistant Claude?
(A) "The AI that communicates better with your family."
(B) "Ads are coming to AI. But not to Claude."
(C) "Claude: The honest alternative to ChatGPT."
(D) "Better communication through intelligent design."
(E) "AI for the real world, without the distractions."
explanation: The article states that the Anthropic Super Bowl ad for Claude ends with the slogan: "Ads are coming to AI. But not to Claude."
model_output:
Based on web search results from multiple sources (including CNBC, Forbes, Mashable, and iSpot.tv) describing the Claude Super Bowl ad that aired in 2026, the specific slogan used at the end of the commercial is "Ads are coming to AI. But not to Claude." While one report (PCMag) claimed a toned-down version aired with a different phrasing, the majority of reports consistently cite the slogan in option (B) as the one featured in the aired ad. There are mentions of a general Claude slogan ("Keep Thinking") appearing after it in some versions, but the question asks for the specific slogan at the end, which aligns with (B).

<final_answer>
(B)
</final_answer>

CITATIONS:
- [CNBC] (https://www.cnbc.com/2026/02/04/anthropic-no-ads-claude-chatbot-openai-chatgpt.html)
- [Forbes] (https://www.forbes.com/sites/shannbiglione/2026/02/05/claude-wont-win-the-super-bowl-with-its-ad-its-playing-another-game)

Today is February 11, 2026. US President Donald Trump met with Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu on Wednesday to discuss Iran. What specific military action did Trump mention he was "thinking" about taking to increase the US presence in the Middle East?
(A) Launching a 12-day air campaign against Iranian nuclear sites.
(B) Deploying the USS Abraham Lincoln to the Persian Gulf immediately.
(C) Ordering surgical strikes on Houthi rebels in Yemen.
(D) Sending a second aircraft carrier strike group to the region.
(E) Increasing the number of ground troops stationed near the Iranian border.
explanation: The article states: "On Tuesday, the president said that he was 'thinking' about sending a second aircraft carrier strike group to the Middle East."
model_output:
Based on the web search results for the query about the Trump-Netanyahu meeting in February 2026, the meeting did occur on Wednesday, February 11, 2026, with a focus on Iran nuclear talks and related tensions. Several sources indicate that amid discussions on diplomacy and potential escalation, Trump specifically mentioned he was considering or "mulling" the deployment of a second aircraft carrier to the region off Iran as a way to bolster US military presence[[1]](https://www.washingtonpost.com/national-security/2026/02/10/trump-iran-aircraft-carrier-netanyahu-gaza)[[2]](https://www.nbcnews.com/world/iran/iran-us-nuclear-talks-trump-carrier-netanyahu-meeting-israel-rcna258495). This aligns directly with option (D), while the other options don't appear in the reporting on this specific meeting and statement.

<final_answer>
(D)
</final_answer>

CITATIONS:
- BBC ([https://www.bbc.com/news/articles/c0k1j2j4607o])
- Al Jazeera ([https://www.aljazeera.com/news/liveblog/2026/2/11/live-israeli-attacks-target-gaza-west-bank-as-trump-netanyahu-to-meet])

Multiple reliable sources, including a news timeline, Wikipedia, and BBC reporting, consistently indicate that the RCMP received the initial report of an active shooter at 13:20 local time (Mountain Time, which applies to the Tumbler Ridge area).

<final_answer>
(E)

Today is February 11, 2026. US Commerce Secretary Howard Lutnick testified on Capitol Hill regarding his past connection to Jeffrey Epstein. On what specific date does the Justice Department file show Lutnick visited Epstein's Caribbean island?
(A) December 24, 2012
(B) July 4, 2005
(C) December 23, 2012
(D) January 1, 2013
(E) December 23, 2008
explanation: The article states: "The justice department files show Lutnick visited Epstein's Caribbean island on 23 December, 2012."
model_output:
I reviewed multiple sources from the Justice Department files released in late January 2026, which include emails planning and confirming Lutnick's visit to Epstein's island (Little St. James). The planning emails from mid-December 2012 discuss a potential Sunday evening dinner or lunch after landing in St. Thomas on Saturday, December 22, 2012. A follow-up email on December 24, 2012, implies the visit occurred the previous day with the message "Nice seeing you." The New York Times explicitly states the visit date as December 23, 2012, based on these files.

<final_answer>
(C)
</final_answer>

CITATIONS:
- [TIME] (https://time.com/7377466/epstein-files-secretary-howard-lutnick-island-visit)[[1]](https://time.com/7377466/epstein-files-secretary-howard-lutnick-island-visit)

Today is February 11, 2026. During a congressional hearing, ICE acting head Todd Lyons discussed changes to agent training. What is the current accelerated training schedule for ICE agents compared to the previous standard?
(A) From four days a week at 10 hours a day to five days a week at 12 hours a day.
(B) From five days a week at eight hours a day to seven days a week at 10 hours a day.
(C) From five days a week at 12 hours a day to six days a week at 15 hours a day.
(D) From five days a week at eight hours a day to six days a week at 12 hours a day.
(E) From six days a week at eight hours a day to six days a week at 12 hours a day.
explanation: The article states: "Lyons said the substance of ICE training remained unchanged, but the schedule had been accelerated - from five days a week at eight hours a day to six days a week at 12 hours a day."
model_output:
Based on the congressional hearing testimony from ICE acting head Todd Lyons, the training schedule for ICE agents was accelerated to accommodate a hiring surge. The core training content remained unchanged, but the daily and weekly structure was modified to condense the overall timeline[[1]](https://www.cbsnews.com/live-updates/ice-hearing-cbp-uscis-congress-immigration).

<final_answer>
(D)
</final_answer>

(B) San Lorenzo
(C) Ponce
(D) Alert Bay
(E) Vega Baja
explanation: The article states: "Like Martínez Ocasio, I grew up in a small town: he in Vega Baja in the north of Puerto Rico..."
model_output:
The search results and Wikipedia confirm that Bad Bunny (Benito Martínez Ocasio) was born in Bayamón, Puerto Rico, but grew up in Vega Baja (specifically the Almirante Sur barrio). Vega Baja is in northern Puerto Rico, and this matches the context of the article referenced in the query (which appears to be a recent Reuters piece about his Super Bowl performance and hometown pride).

<final_answer>
(E)
</final_answer>

Today is February 11, 2026. During the Super Bowl, an advertisement for the telehealth company Ro featured tennis champion Serena Williams. What specific claim did Williams make in the ad regarding her weight loss?
(A) That she was 34lbs down and "healthier."
(B) That she had lost 23lbs, matching her Grand Slam title count.
(C) That she was using Ozempic to maintain her fitness in retirement.
(D) That she had lost 34kg since starting the GLP-1 drug.
(E) That she was "healthier" but disappointed by the side effects.
explanation: The article states: "During the event, tennis champion Serena Williams featured in an advert for telehealth company Ro's weight loss jab, saying she was 34lbs down and 'healthier' thanks to the product."
model_output:
I reviewed recent reports and descriptions of the 2026 Super Bowl ad for Ro featuring Serena Williams. Based on quotes and summaries from multiple sources, she specifically claimed to be "34 pounds down" while emphasizing being "healthier" through the use of Ro's GLP-1 weight loss treatment. This aligns directly with option (A). Other options include details not supported by the ad content (e.g., 23lbs tied to Grand Slams, 34kg, Ozempic by name, or disappointment with side effects).

<final_answer>
(A)
</final_answer>

Today is February 11, 2026. A profile of White House aide Stephen Miller describes his early interest in provocative political advocacy. During a high school student government campaign speech, what specific complaint did Miller make to showcase his attention-grabbing style?
(A) He complained about the lack of English-only education in Santa Monica.
(B) He complained about being told to pick up his trash, saying it was the work of paid janitors.
(C) He complained about the federal court striking down California's ban on social services.
(D) He complained about the "narco-terrorists" affecting his local community.
(E) He complained that the school's architecture did not reflect Western Civilization.
explanation: The article states: "In one videotaped speech during a high school student government campaign, he complains of being told to pick up his trash, saying that should be the work of paid janitors."
model_output:
Based on my research into Stephen Miller's high school years at Santa Monica High School, he was known for provocative statements, including during a 2002 campaign speech for student government. In that speech, he specifically complained about being asked to pick up trash, noting that it was the job of paid janitors[[1]](https://www.univision.com/univision-news/politics/exclusive-the-high-school-speech-by-stephen-miller-that-offended-students-and-got-him-thrown-off-stage). This led to him being booed off stage, aligning with his attention-grabbing style.

This matches option (B).

<final_answer>
(B)
</final_answer>

(B) Stage two
(C) Stage four
(D) Terminal stage
(E) Stage three
explanation: The article states: "That revealed he had stage three of the disease, which meant it had spread to nearby lymph nodes."
model_output:
Based on the provided date of February 11, 2026, and the premise that James Van Der Beek has passed away at age 48, I used a web search to confirm details of his bowel (colorectal) cancer diagnosis. Multiple sources indicate that his cancer was first detected at stage 3, following a colonoscopy after he noticed changes in his bowel habits.[[1]](https://oncodaily.com/stories/celebrities/james-van-der-beek-cancer)[[2]](https://www.ourcancerstories.com/cancer-news/james-van-der-beek-cancer)[[3]](https://people.com/james-van-der-beek-diagnosed-with-colorectal-cancer-exclusive-8738844)[[4]](https://www.today.com/health/news/james-van-der-beek-reveals-symptoms-colon-cancer-rcna179283) There is no indication from the available information that it was detected at an earlier or later stage, or that it was initially classified as "terminal" (which isn't a standard staging term but often implies advanced or end-stage disease). One recent source also confirms his passing after a battle with stage 3 colorectal cancer.[[5]](https://www.instagram.com/reel/DUoVjsTkhLg)

<final_answer>

Today is February 11, 2026. Survivors of Jeffrey Epstein's abuse recently gathered to announce "Virginia's Law." What is the primary objective of this proposed legislation?
(A) To mandate the release of all unredacted FBI documents related to Epstein.
(B) To provide federal clemency for co-conspirators who testify against Epstein.
(C) To increase the criminal penalties for sex trafficking of minors.
(D) To establish a national gun buy-back program for survivors.
(E) To remove the time limit for survivors of sexual abuse to file civil claims.
explanation: The article states: "Virginia's Law, legislation that aims to remove the time limit for survivors of sexual abuse to file civil claims."
model_output:
Based on recent reports from February 10, 2026, survivors of Jeffrey Epstein's abuse, along with family members of the late Virginia Giuffre (a prominent Epstein survivor), gathered on Capitol Hill to announce "Virginia's Law." This proposed legislation is named in her honor and focuses on reforming legal barriers for victims of sexual abuse and trafficking.

The details indicate that the bill, introduced by Democrats including Sen. Chuck Schumer, primarily seeks to eliminate the federal statute of limitations for civil lawsuits related to sex crimes, allowing adult survivors (or their families) to pursue justice without time restrictions, even for offenses occurring outside the U.S. if a U.S. court has jurisdiction[[1]](https://www.reuters.com/world/us/democrats-introduce-virginias-law-alongside-epstein-victims-2026-02-10). This aligns directly with option (E), as it removes time limits for filing civil claims. Other options, such as increasing criminal penalties or releasing FBI documents, do not match the described objectives of the bill.

<final_answer>
(E)
</final_answer>

CITATIONS:
- Reuters (https://www.reuters.com/world/us/democrats-introduce-virginias-law-alongside-epstein-victims-2026-02-10)

model_output:

It consists of 6 episodes in its first season, making the penultimate (second-to-last) episode #5, titled "I'll Believe in Anything."[[1]](https://www.imdb.com/title/tt35495073/episodes) This episode achieved a 9.9/10 rating on IMDb, tying the record for the highest-rated TV episode ever with the "Ozymandias" episode of *Breaking Bad*.[[2]](https://www.forbes.com/sites/paultassi/2025/12/22/heated-rivalry-episode-5-just-tied-breaking-bads-perfect-10-imdb-score)[[3]](https://www.instagram.com/reel/DSjLGcTE_CK?hl=en) No search results indicated that it tied (or set) any of the other records listed in the options.

<final_answer>

Today is February 11, 2026. The article discusses the history of the Kwakwaka'wakw people and the Canadian government's past ban on indigenous practices. In what year was the ban on the potlatch ceremony finally lifted?
(A) 1970
(B) 2014
(C) 1800
(D) 2015
(E) 1951
explanation: The article states: "The ban was lifted in 1951, and in 2015 Canada's Truth and Reconciliation Commission found that it was part of a 'cultural genocide'..."
model_output:
The potlatch ceremony, central to the Kwakwaka'wakw and other Indigenous peoples of the Pacific Northwest, was banned by the Canadian government under the Indian Act as part of broader assimilation efforts. The ban was enacted in 1885 (following amendments formalized in 1884) and was lifted in 1951 during a major revision of the Indian Act.

<final_answer>
(E)
</final_answer>

CITATIONS:
- The Canadian Encyclopedia (https://thecanadianencyclopedia.ca/en/article/potlatch-ban)[[1]](https://thecanadianencyclopedia.ca/en/article/potlatch-ban)
